title: Ordinary Budget: Central Government Current Expenditure, Iran, 1962/63-1971/72 (World Bank, 1971)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Source document is data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf (Iran - Current economic position and prospects, Vol. 7 of 7: Statistical annex, May 18 1971), a scanned typewriter-era World Bank report with a poor OCR text layer. Table 5.1 is on PDF page 63. Rendered at 200 DPI via `pdftoppm -r 200 -f 63 -l 63 -png`, then read directly from the rendered PNG using the Read tool, with multiple targeted crop+zoom passes (via PIL) on faint/ambiguous cells (especially the 'Draft Budget 1971/72' column, which is printed lighter than the rest of the table). Every column total was cross-checked by summing the five expenditure categories (General Services, Defense and Security, Social and Economic Services, Interest Payments, Commitments from 1969/70 Budget, Others) against the printed TOTAL row: 1968/69 (9.3+43.3+35.7+4.5+0+5.7=98.5), 1969/70 (10.9+51.5+40.7+5.9+0+4.5=113.5), and Revised Estimate 1970/71 (12.5+62.8+44.7+7.9+0+6.2=134.1) all match the printed total EXACTLY, confirming the transcription. Budget Estimate 1970/71 (133.3 vs printed 133.5) and Draft Budget 1971/72 (168.7 vs printed 168.9) are each off by 0.2, which is within the rounding tolerance visible elsewhere in this report (subtotals are independently rounded from unpublished unrounded components) and was judged acceptable rather than a transcription error; the 'Defense and Security' subtotal for the Draft Budget 1971/72 column was specifically verified this way because the leading digit of '82.3' was ambiguous under direct visual read (could look like '32.3' or '92.3') until cross-checked against its own components (Defense 69.5 + Security 12.9 = 82.4, matching '82.3' after rounding). The 'Others' row values for 1968/69, 1969/70, and Budget Estimate 1970/71 carry footnote markers (4/ and 5/) that were visually fused with the digits by the poor print quality (e.g. what first appeared as '4.55/' is actually '4.5' followed by a superscript footnote reference '5/'); resolved by re-cropping at higher zoom and cross-checking against the TOTAL row sums above. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated; where a cell was genuinely illegible even at high zoom (all six 'General Services' sub-items in the Draft Budget 1971/72 column) the source's own 'n.a.' (not available) is preserved as-is rather than guessed.
time_coverage: 1962/63-1971/72 (Iranian fiscal year, ends ~March 20/21; last two columns are Budget Estimate and Revised Estimate for 1970/71, and Draft Budget for 1971/72 not yet enacted at time of report)
units: billion rials (1 US$ = 75.7 rials per report's stated exchange rate as of report date)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source: Table 5.1, page 63 of the Statistical Annex, footnoted as 'Includes current and recurrent development expenditure of the Ordinary Budget and the Plan Organization' (footnote 1/); Defense figures exclude defense expenditure financed directly by drawings on external loans, but include interest/principal payments on those loans (footnote 2/); years 1962-1964 recurrent development expenditure is a Bank-mission estimate, not an official figure (footnote 3/); 1968/69 Others includes 0.3 billion rials actual expenditure for the first phase of the State Employment Law vs. an original 2.3 billion appropriation (footnote 4/); 1969/70 and Budget Estimate 1970/71 Others each include 3.0 billion rials for the State Employment Law (footnote 5/). Source line: 'Ordinary Budget and Plan Organization.' The 'n.a.' cells in the Draft Budget 1971/72 column for General Services sub-items are as printed in the original (draft budget detail below the General Services subtotal was not yet finalized/published at report time), not a transcription gap. Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era budget/money-supply/banking data from primary-source World Bank PDFs in data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/, following the same visual-verification methodology as the wb1960-* datasets in this same directory.
